word.item: violet. The root was used medicinally, but Sesostrie doesn't know how or what for.
where is the wood? (same thing, only different word, says Sesostrie)
you cutting the tree, a tree?
where is the ocean? (volunteered not so good)
where is the coffee? (also said: ta´kafi)
where is the horn? (corrected to ʔo´ner)
the fingers are lying (good: suggested by some)
the trees (d.) are standing, lento with (u); allegro without
where is the man lying down?
he ran (il a curü u tʌ)
when lightening strikes a tree, it burns
"red jack", has a red root, thick like potatoes, and when you boil it the water is red, red. Used as a medicine - for bowel diseases - good for purifying the blood, "thin it out" (says N.)
they make him miserable, i.e., "punish him".
(Sesostrie called Alexandre's daughter this, Sesostrie called Marceline's daughter this)
it's very muddy (= there's lots of mud)
come! (f.) - here, better than both the other forms, more polite
to a woman You must come.
cuss word (I hate you) (not said to a child) (use this word when you're mad)
I'm going to whip you (to female)
I hate you, come here! (pa´na man is one phrase?)
He push me, so I pinched her. He make me that way, I pinched her.
I wan tto try to smoke
he put the bridle on (a horse)
the boy's father killed a white rabbit
wait! (to either m. or w.) (?) (don't move!)
you are happy m. d. (can't pronounce it)
you are happy f. d. (can't pronounce it)
They done eating, i.e. they have eaten
I want to go see her
when you do good for a person
cercelle (sic) something like a mallard, but smaller. In all probability, the green-winged teal.
fly-catcher, literally, and possibly to be questioned ą̈ perar
you (m.) are going to hear it
you (f.) are going to hear it
he is going to hear it
she is going to hear it
wood-thrush (thrushes, hermit, wood, and gray-checked, mostly like a wood-thrush)
a kind of animal that stays in the water
lives in the water, a small turtle
garfish (about big as a man's leg)
another kind of garfish (4-5 feet long)
dèʒagàsya´ (French), used to make bows with this, possibly bowdark
tree that has little red seed
root used for dying cane red
where is the hill (about the same thing)

interlinear-text.item: Creole French words at top of page?

word.item: when the Tunica and the Biloxi
what day you going to come back?
I'm coming back day after tomorrow
